steps.title: Find the sample proportion
steps.content:
• The sample contains 4 fertilized lawns out of 20 total.
  • Write the proportion as a fraction and reduce: $$ \frac{4}{20} = \frac{1}{5} = 0.2 $$
  • Thus the proportion of fertilized lawns in the sample is 0.2.

steps.title: Scale the proportion to the whole town
steps.content:
• Multiply the sample proportion by the total number of lawns: $$ 0.2 \times 260 = 52 $$
  • The best estimate for the number of fertilized lawns in the town is 52.
  • That matches choice D — D is correct.
